data.content:
# Implementation Plan: Progressive Loading UX for All Repositories Mode

## Architecture Overview

The streaming infrastructure (knot bf85) already delivers NDJSON chunks per-repo through fetchBeatsForScopeStreaming(). The onRepoChunk callback in fetchBeatsWithStreaming() merges beats incrementally via queryClient.setQueryData(). This plan adds the UX layer on top: tracking streaming progress, showing visual feedback, and smoothing data insertion.

## Key Insight

The current onRepoChunk callback only receives (repo, repoName, beats) -- there is no metadata about total repo count or which repos are pending. We need to: (1) extend the streaming state to track per-repo completion, (2) expose this state to UI components through a new hook/store, (3) build UI components that consume this state.

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: Streaming Progress State (new file + hook changes)

New file: src/app/beats/use-streaming-progress.ts
- Create StreamingProgress type: { totalRepos, loadedRepos, isStreaming, isComplete }
- Create useStreamingProgress() hook backed by useRef
- Returns: { progress, onStreamStart, onRepoLoaded, onStreamComplete, resetProgress }

Modify: src/app/beats/use-beats-query.ts
- Call onStreamStart(registeredRepos.length) before streaming fetch
- Call onRepoLoaded(repo) in onRepoChunk callback
- Call onStreamComplete() after fetch resolves
- Export StreamingProgress from UseBeatsQueryResult

### Step 2: Per-Repo Loading Progress Indicator (new component)

New file: src/components/streaming-progress-bar.tsx
- Thin animated progress bar + text: "Loaded 3/5 repositories"
- Respects prefers-reduced-motion
- Fades out when isComplete becomes true
- Only renders in scope=all mode

### Step 3: Skeleton/Shimmer Rows

New file: src/components/beat-table-skeleton-rows.tsx
- 3 shimmer rows matching table column structure
- animate-pulse with prefers-reduced-motion disabled variant

### Step 4: Smooth Data Insertion with Fade-In Animation

Modify: src/app/globals.css
- Add @keyframes beat-row-enter (opacity 0->1, 200ms)
- Add prefers-reduced-motion media query

Modify: src/components/beat-table-content.tsx
- Track newly inserted beat IDs via useRef<Set<string>>
- Apply animate-beat-row-enter class to new rows

### Step 5: Stable Scroll Position During Insertion

Modify: src/components/beat-table.tsx
- Accept optional isStreaming prop
- Skip setPageIdx(0) reset when isStreaming and data length increases

### Step 6: Completion Signal

Part of streaming-progress-bar.tsx:
- Text changes to "All repositories loaded"
- Bar fills to 100% then fades out after 1.5s

### Step 7: Empty State Distinction

Modify: src/app/beats/page.tsx -- BeatsListContent
- Streaming active + no beats: "Loading repositories..." (not "No beats found")
- Streaming complete + no beats: "No results found across all repositories."
- Single-repo: unchanged

### Step 8: Wire Everything Together

Modify: src/app/beats/page.tsx
- Thread streamingProgress through to BeatsListContent
- Render StreamingProgressBar above table when streaming
- Render BeatTableSkeletonRows below table when streaming

## File Change Summary

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| src/app/beats/use-streaming-progress.ts | New -- progress tracking hook |
| src/components/streaming-progress-bar.tsx | New -- progress bar + completion |
| src/components/beat-table-skeleton-rows.tsx | New -- shimmer rows |
| src/app/beats/use-beats-query.ts | Modify -- integrate callbacks |
| src/app/beats/page.tsx | Modify -- wire state, conditional rendering |
| src/components/beat-table.tsx | Modify -- isStreaming prop, suppress reset |
| src/components/beat-table-content.tsx | Modify -- fade-in animation |
| src/app/globals.css | Modify -- keyframes + reduced-motion |

## Test Strategy

Unit tests:
- use-streaming-progress.test.ts -- progress state transitions
- streaming-progress-bar.test.tsx -- render states, completion
- beat-table-skeleton-rows.test.tsx -- correct rendering

Behavioral:
- Single-repo mode shows no streaming progress UI
- Empty state text varies by streaming state

## Complexity: Medium
## Risks: TanStack Table re-sort cost per setQueryData call (mitigated by per-repo batching), animation jank on low-end devices (mitigated by prefers-reduced-motion)
## Dependencies: knot bf85 (already shipped), no new packages needed
